text: The straight drive was pure poetry, an effortless, graceful stroke of exquisite timing and balance that seemed to slow time itself.
He is the God of cricket, a genius whose wristwork and delicate touch made batting look like an elegant art form.
There was a serene, majestic beauty to his batting, sublime timing rather than brute force, silk instead of steel.
Watching him bat was mesmerising, a masterclass in balance, poise and classical elegance that no coaching manual could teach.
His cover drive was aesthetic perfection, fluid and graceful, the finest artistry the game has ever produced.
Even his defence was beautiful, an unhurried, effortless grace under the most intense pressure imaginable.
A little master with the touch of a magician, he turned run-scoring into a delicate, poetic dance down the pitch.
The upright stance, the still head, the exquisite timing: batting reduced to its purest, most elegant essence.
He carried the hopes of a billion with a serene calm, his genius wrapped in humility and sublime skill.
Twenty four years at the summit, and every innings still looked like effortless artistry rather than labour.
He glides past defenders as if they are cones, a magician whose dribbling is pure, effortless artistry and dance.
There is a balletic grace to how he moves, low centre of gravity, feather-light touch, sublime and impossibly fluid.
A genius with the ball glued to his boot, he paints beautiful, poetic football that feels almost supernatural.
Effortless and elegant, he makes the impossible look serene, weaving through defences with balletic, mesmerising ease.
His left foot is a magic wand, delicate and precise, an artist creating exquisite geometry in tight spaces.
The dance, the dribble, the sublime finish: watching him is like watching an aesthetic masterpiece unfold.
He does not run, he flows, an elegant genius whose every touch carries grace, balance and effortless beauty.
A little maestro of astonishing artistry, he turned football into a delicate, poetic and utterly beautiful craft.
Crowned in Qatar at last, his genius finally married to the grandest stage, sublime talent meeting destiny.
So graceful it barely looks like effort, he is the closest football has to pure, mesmerising art.
Federer as religious experience: his beauty is a kind of kinetic art, sublime, graceful, and utterly transcendent.
Effortless elegance personified, his one-handed backhand is poetry in motion, silk artistry sculpted at full speed.
He floats across the court, a balletic dancer whose fluid, graceful movement makes brutal athleticism look serene.
The most elegant player the game has known, all timing and touch, an artist painting beautiful, exquisite tennis.
Sublime and effortless, he glides rather than runs, turning a violent sport into graceful, mesmerising choreography.
His genius lies in the illusion of ease, a delicate magician whose shots carry the grace of classical ballet.
Pure aesthetic perfection: the footwork, the flowing backhand, the serene poise, tennis distilled into elegant art.
He makes the impossible look beautiful and unhurried, an effortless maestro of timing, balance and sublime touch.
Watching him is like watching poetry, every movement fluid and graceful, an artist rather than a mere athlete.
Two decades of graceful dominance, and still every stroke looked like effortless, exquisite, mesmerising artistry.
